subject: Databases
title: The N+1 Query Problem
description: A quick, practical guide to The N+1 Query Problem.

hook.text: Load 100 orders, then fetch the customer for each one. That's 101 queries instead of 1. The N+1 problem.

buildup.text: ORMs hide this: order.customer triggers a query per iteration. It looks clean but performs terribly.

discovery.text: Fix it with eager loading: fetch all 100 orders and their customers in two queries, not 101.

twist.text: A JOIN can do it in one query. But ORMs default to lazy loading, which creates N+1 silently.

climax.text: N+1 is the most common ORM performance bug. Use query logging to detect it early.

punchline.text: N+1: innocent-looking code, disastrous performance. Always check.

quiz.question: How does eager loading solve the N+1 query problem?
options: It fetches all related records in one or two queries instead of one per record
It caches all queries in memory forever
It disables the ORM and uses raw SQL
